title: SoundHound AI (SOUN) Q3 2025 Earnings Transcript
text: The transcript documents SoundHound AI's third-quarter 2025 earnings call, featuring remarks from CEO Keyvan Mohajer, CFO Nitesh Sharan, and Chief Communications Officer Scott Smith. Key Financial Highlights: Q3 revenue reached $42 million, representing 68% year-over-year growth. Year-to-date revenue totaled $114 million, up 127%. Full-year 2025 guidance raised to $165-$180 million. Gross margin improved to 43% (59% non-GAAP). Cash position of $269 million with zero debt. Major Business Developments: The company announced completion of the Interactions acquisition and signed a significant IoT deal with a Chinese manufacturer involving double-digit millions of smart devices over two to three years. Management emphasized that the vast majority of our revenue is recurring, with diversified contract structures. Product Innovation: SoundHound highlighted its Polaris foundation model achieving a threefold reduction in error rates for transitioning clients. The Amelia 7 platform migration targets approximately 75% customer adoption by mid-2026, with voice commerce production launches expected in 2026 across four OEMs. Customer Expansion: Notable wins included partnerships with major financial institutions, automotive manufacturers, and enterprise clients across healthcare, retail, energy, and insurance sectors, with no single customer exceeding 10% of revenue.
